title: rhododendron ellipticum var. tanakae
intro: Rhododendron ellipticum var. tanakae, also known as Tanaka's rhododendron, is a variety of the elliptical leaved rhododendron, a flowering plant native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds. The health benefits of Tanaka's rhododendron include treating fever, sore throats, and other infections, as well as providing relief from rheumatism and other inflammatory conditions. Herbal preparations of Tanaka's rhododendron, such as infusions and tinctures, can be made by steeping the leaves in hot water or mixing the plant material with a solvent like ethanol to extract its medicinal compounds.
